page: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration.

This instance is highly optimized for network and EBS performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in specific areas, including a Passmark CPU score of 48,648.63 and a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 43,676.13, both placing in the top 25% tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 40.89 GB/sec. For language model tasks, prompt processing with a small 135M model reaches a throughput of 2,446.12 tokens/sec, while text generation for the same model averages 250.84 tokens/sec.

The c6in.12xlarge presents distinct architectural tradeoffs. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes, which is mitigated by the high 75 Gbps network baseline. The memory allocation of 2.0 GB per vCPU is tailored for compute-heavy workloads rather than memory-intensive databases. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high resource density for compute-bound tasks, leveraging advanced vector extensions and cryptography accelerators to maximize processing throughput per allocated resource.

This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from high network throughput and dedicated CPU performance. Appropriate use cases include high-performance web serving, software compilation, image processing, network appliances, and distributed analytics or batch processing where high-speed interconnects are required.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, resulting in a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage supported by a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong results in Passmark CPU tests, static web serving at 40.89 GB/sec, and image processing. It is highly suited for compute-bound and network-intensive workloads such as high-performance web serving, software compilation, and cryptography tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 75 Gbps network baseline, designed for high-throughput network and processing workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized, network-enhanced instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed baseline bandwidth for demanding processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage
Strong performance in static web serving and cryptographic operations
Best suited for compute-bound, network-intensive, and batch processing workloads
